text.title: PHP Eloquent ORM Patterns Examples
text.summary: Worked example: harden Order model with $fillable, $casts, scope, and transactional create.

example.context: Laravel 11; Order model has no $fillable, no $casts; OrderController::store does Order::create($request->all()) + InventoryItem::decrement separately.
example.walk: Step 1: `protected $fillable = ['customer_id', 'items', 'note'];` + `protected $casts = ['items' => 'array', 'paid_at' => 'datetime', 'total_cents' => 'integer'];`. Step 2: `scopeRecent($q) { return $q->where('created_at', '>=', now()->subDays(30))); }` + scopePaid. Step 3: audit → 2 N+1 sites in OrderResource (customer + items); add `Order::with(['customer', 'items'])->get()` at service. Step 4: OrderService::create wraps both Order::create + Inventory decrement in DB::transaction. Step 5: tests assert that POST /orders with `is_admin=1` in body is ignored; assertQueryCount=3 on list endpoint; throw mid-tx leaves DB clean.
example.output: Mass-assignment safe; N+1 eliminated; multi-write atomic.
